title: The Wandering Juvie

jokes.text: where are your clothes for the gentleman who's big and fat? The basement, sir.
jokes.setup: Homer asking where plus-size clothing is located
jokes.punchline: Employee matter-of-factly directing the fat customer to the basement
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Homer - the blunt, matter-of-fact response creates awkward humor perfectly suited to his character

jokes.text: damn this-- oh!-- Resilient economy! Oh!
jokes.setup: Homer cursing about something frustrating
jokes.punchline: Mid-curse he switches to praising the economy for no logical reason
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection where Homer's complaint transforms into praise mid-sentence - absurd and economical

jokes.text: this is our 'li'l hooker' line. All the girls your age are wearing it-- except the freakishly unpopular.
jokes.setup: Employee selling clothing to young Lisa
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately named clothing line plus brutal assessment of unpopular kids
jokes.explanation: Dark humor combining inappropriate product naming with casual cruelty about unpopular children

jokes.text: He's eating my beauty! Mm-hmm. Skin mites love the taste of beauty.
jokes.setup: Lisa reacting to microscopic view of her skin
jokes.punchline: Employee's matter-of-fact explanation that mites literally consume beauty
jokes.explanation: Absurd personification of skin mites as beauty-consumers, delivered with deadpan authority

jokes.text: I hope he didn't have children. Do they have children? Millions of them.
jokes.setup: Lisa hoping the skin mite didn't reproduce
jokes.punchline: Not only do they reproduce, but in massive numbers
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke - Lisa's worst fear confirmed and amplified

jokes.text: sir, other customers need to use that dressing room. Dressing room? Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Employee asking Homer to vacate dressing room
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion reveals he didn't realize he was in a dressing room
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - he's been using the dressing room for something else entirely

jokes.text: And I suppose those wedding guests will also pay for dishwashing liquid, heated water, and two-sided sponges?
jokes.setup: Edna explaining wedding registry benefits
jokes.punchline: Skinner's pedantic breakdown of hidden dishwashing costs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character work - his penny-pinching mentality applied to wedding gifts with bureaucratic precision

jokes.text: Silent anger: The cornerstone of a successful marriage.
jokes.setup: Edna giving him silent treatment
jokes.punchline: Skinner treating passive aggression as relationship wisdom
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure and economy - takes dysfunctional relationship dynamic and frames it as marriage advice with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Cool. Guns. Die, happy couples.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing what he thinks are weapons
jokes.punchline: His immediate violent fantasy targeting wedding couples
jokes.explanation: Bart's characteristic mischievous violence directed at symbols of adult happiness

jokes.text: that's not a toy, son. It's a bar code reader. It registers wedding gifts. Bull-honky!
jokes.setup: Employee explaining the scanner's mundane purpose
jokes.punchline: Bart's incredulous rejection of boring reality
jokes.explanation: Bart's childish curse word 'bull-honky' and refusal to accept mundane explanations

jokes.text: I'm going to the employee lounge to finish my shasta.
jokes.setup: Employee leaving to attend to personal business
jokes.punchline: Specifically mentioning the discount soda brand
jokes.explanation: Mundane detail about cheap soda brand suggests low-wage retail life

jokes.text: Name of groom. Bart simpson. Name of bride. Lotta cooties.
jokes.setup: Bart filling out fake wedding registry
jokes.punchline: His childish view of girls as disease carriers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - his immature understanding of relationships expressed through playground terminology

jokes.text: a fake wedding? That's what I call chutzpah! Yeah. I'm gonna scam this town out of so many presents.
jokes.setup: Milhouse admiring Bart's fake wedding scheme
jokes.punchline: Bart's gleeful criminal planning
jokes.explanation: Standard Bart scheme setup with Milhouse as admiring accomplice

jokes.text: And what I don't use, I'm gonna return for store credit! Both: Store credit! Store credit! Store credit!
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his gift return strategy
jokes.punchline: Both kids chanting about store credit like it's treasure
jokes.explanation: Kids' excitement over mundane adult concept of store credit

jokes.text: Another employee family wedding. What's the traditional peasant gift in these parts, a milking cow?
jokes.setup: Burns learning about employee wedding
jokes.punchline: His condescending assumption about working-class gift traditions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character work - his elitist condescension toward employees expressed through antiquated rural stereotypes

jokes.text: Remember, if anyone asks, you're my niece from out of town. I am your niece, uncle joe. Good lord, I'm an abomination!
jokes.setup: Bart coaching Milhouse on fake identity
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's sudden horrified self-realization
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's neurotic self-awareness about his feminine appearance in drag

jokes.text: this prank is my sergeant pepper'S.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on his fake wedding scheme
jokes.punchline: Comparing his prank to the Beatles' masterpiece album
jokes.explanation: Bart's grandiose self-regard expressed through high-culture reference - treating juvenile prank as artistic achievement

jokes.text: I see neither blushing bride, nor ardent swain.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy arriving at fake wedding
jokes.punchline: His unnecessarily flowery vocabulary for simple observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy pretentiousness - using archaic romantic language where simple words would suffice

jokes.text: oh, my god, we're sealed in! Hey, nobody breathe my air! Get your own!
jokes.setup: Panic about being trapped in store
jokes.punchline: Immediate territorial claim over breathable air
jokes.explanation: Panic-induced selfishness taken to absurd extreme - hoarding air

jokes.text: Would some flatware make things right? Um, what does it say on my badge? 'Cash bribes only.'
jokes.setup: Bart attempting to bribe his way out of arrest
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's badge explicitly states his bribery preferences
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with perfect Wiggum character work - corruption so blatant it's advertised on his badge

jokes.text: Bart, the record of your mischief is staggering. Just look at this file. That doesn't look so big. These are directions to the facility where bart's criminal record occupies three full storage lockers.
jokes.setup: Judge showing Bart's criminal file, Bart thinks it's small
jokes.punchline: The 'file' is just directions to where his massive record is stored
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection escalation - what appears small is actually directions to something enormous

jokes.text: Six feet by eight? Six by 14.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about storage locker dimensions
jokes.punchline: Even bigger than he imagined
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of the storage locker gag

jokes.text: Please, judge, you can'T. I-I'll do anything. I'll squeal on my dad-- he's been up to bad things. Crap you never even thought of!
jokes.setup: Bart desperately trying to avoid juvie
jokes.punchline: Immediately offering to betray his father with enthusiastic detail
jokes.explanation: Bart's family loyalty instantly crumbling under pressure, with hint of Homer's unknown crimes

jokes.text: We've already got an informer working deep cover on your dad. One he'll never suspect. Is it lenny? Damn it! I mean... uh, no.
jokes.setup: Judge reveals they have an informant on Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately guesses correctly, judge accidentally confirms
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with Bart's lucky guess exposing the judge's incompetence

jokes.text: No! You're not going to take my baby to jail! all right, boys, she's not letting go. Make the switch.
jokes.setup: Marge clinging to Bart to prevent arrest
jokes.punchline: Police have a procedure for switching out arrested children
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag implying police have dealt with this exact situation before

jokes.text: your eyes need diapers. 'Your eyes need di...' that's good, ralphie.
jokes.setup: Someone crying heavily
jokes.punchline: Ralph's bizarre but oddly poetic observation about tears
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum absurdist logic - his childish misunderstanding creates unexpectedly poetic imagery

jokes.text: Perfect mood-setter for the end of your freedom.
jokes.setup: Guard commenting on Bart's harmonica
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically noting its appropriateness for incarceration
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about imprisonment with deadpan delivery from authority figure

jokes.text: It's not all your fault. All these years, I watched you turn our son into a time bomb, and yet I did nothing. So in a way, I, too, am a victim... of you.
jokes.setup: Homer seeming to take responsibility for Bart's problems
jokes.punchline: Turning his confession into blame for Marge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - appearing to take responsibility while actually shifting all blame to Marge

jokes.text: His acting out was probably caused by negative reinforcement. Oh, I get it. Blame the strangler.
jokes.setup: Lisa offering psychological explanation for Bart's behavior
jokes.punchline: Sideshow Bob defensively taking it personally
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Bob's ongoing vendetta against Bart, with his defensive reaction to psychology

jokes.text: these are all fast food jobs. Ex-cons are a godsend to employers like me. If you ask for a raise, I just call your parole officer and back in the can you go!
jokes.setup: Career counselor showing job opportunities to juvie kids
jokes.punchline: Employer gleefully explaining how to exploit ex-convicts
jokes.explanation: Dark satire about exploitation of ex-convicts in the job market

jokes.text: ah, cartoons. America's only native art form. I don't count jazz, 'cause it sucks.
jokes.setup: Character making cultural observation about cartoons
jokes.punchline: Dismissing jazz with crude simplicity
jokes.explanation: Misdirection from highbrow cultural commentary to crude dismissal

jokes.text: Better stay out of the sandbox, I could be buried alive. Maybe the slide...
jokes.setup: Bart trying to find safe playground activities in juvie
jokes.punchline: His paranoid assessment of typical playground equipment as deadly
jokes.explanation: Bart's survival instincts applied to innocent playground equipment

jokes.text: I'm gina. You touch my fence again and your puberty's going to be very boring.
jokes.setup: Gina introducing herself to Bart
jokes.punchline: Creative threat involving bodily harm to his development
jokes.explanation: Excellent threatening one-liner with creative euphemism for castration - economical and memorable

jokes.text: God, I hate this place. It's nothing like the brochure.
jokes.setup: Bart expressing misery about juvie
jokes.punchline: Implying juvenile detention centers have promotional brochures
jokes.explanation: Absurd implication that prison facilities use marketing materials like vacation resorts

jokes.text: They're not true friends. Time's up!
jokes.setup: Marge giving Bart advice about avoiding violent people
jokes.punchline: Visit immediately ended, highlighting the irony of her advice in prison
jokes.explanation: Ironic timing of guard ending visit just as Marge gives useless advice about avoiding violent people in prison

jokes.text: I believe that children are the future, unless we stop them now.
jokes.setup: Marge seemingly quoting inspirational Whitney Houston song lyric
jokes.punchline: Turning it into dystopian threat about controlling children
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection taking inspirational pop culture quote and twisting it into authoritarian threat with perfect economy

jokes.text: This end's for beating, this end's for holding. Uh-huh. When does training start? It just finished.
jokes.setup: Trainer showing Marge how to use nightstick, Marge asks about training
jokes.punchline: The entire training was just that brief explanation
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline about inadequate guard training with economical delivery

jokes.text: If you're going to pick on someone, why don't you pick on someone much bigger than you with a gun?
jokes.setup: Homer confronting bullies picking on Bart
jokes.punchline: His illogical challenge for them to pick on someone more dangerous
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - his protective instincts expressed through backwards reasoning

jokes.text: His dad's a screw? I sure am.
jokes.setup: Kid using prison slang for guard
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly confirming with the literal meaning
jokes.explanation: Homer missing the prison slang meaning and responding to literal interpretation

jokes.text: Oh, wait, monday's martin luther king day. Oh, I'll see you when I see you.
jokes.setup: Homer telling Bart he'll see him Monday
jokes.punchline: Realizing the holiday and becoming vague about when he'll return
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual approach to his parenting responsibilities

jokes.text: Studies show the part of the brain that remembers dance steps is also the anger center. So, juveniles who know how to fox-trot are 10% less likely to commit a double homicide.
jokes.setup: Warden explaining scientific rationale for dance therapy
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated correlation between ballroom dancing and murder rates
jokes.explanation: Absurd pseudo-science connecting elegant dancing to violent crime prevention with specific statistics

jokes.text: Who conducted this study? The institute of 'shut your fat face'!
jokes.setup: Student questioning the validity of the dance study
jokes.punchline: Warden's crude dismissal disguised as academic institution
jokes.explanation: Great authority figure response - disguising 'shut up' as fake academic source

jokes.text: Then you'll have punch and you'll drink it. Then your eyes will meet, and it will be awkward. So help me god.
jokes.setup: Warden commanding students to dance
jokes.punchline: Dictating even the romantic awkwardness with religious oath
jokes.explanation: Warden's authoritarian micromanagement extending to emotional experiences

jokes.text: Okay, here's my rules. Hands at 10:00 and 2:00, no eye contact, and I don't want to hear how pretty I look. Don't worry, you won'T.
jokes.setup: Gina setting strict boundaries for dancing
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt assurance she won't get compliments
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Gina's vanity met with Bart's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Well, I can close it faster, or I can close it right. Can't you do both? Talk to the union.
jokes.setup: Someone asking Homer to close the gate faster during escape
jokes.punchline: Homer invoking union rules to justify poor performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer work ethic - using union protections to justify incompetence during crisis

jokes.text: well, you do look pretty in the moonlight.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment during escape
jokes.punchline: Bart contradicting his earlier blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke where Bart said Gina wouldn't get compliments

jokes.text: guess where the next one's going. Up my butt?
jokes.setup: Gina threatening Bart with something unspecified
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact guess about anal assault
jokes.explanation: Dark prison humor with Bart's casual acceptance of potential sexual violence

jokes.text: Why did you kiss me? Are you looking to do the bartman?
jokes.setup: Bart confused about Gina's romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Reference to his signature dance move as euphemism for sex
jokes.explanation: Clever callback using Bart's signature dance as sexual euphemism

jokes.text: that mean boy... he dragged me out of the dance. I didn't want to go, but he was too strong. But you got 20 pounds on me. And then he said I was fat.
jokes.setup: Gina giving fake victim story to authorities
jokes.punchline: Bart pointing out physical impossibility, Gina adding insult to story
jokes.explanation: Gina's manipulative lying skills versus Bart's logical objections

jokes.text: But I have to wizzle. Fine. I miss prison.
jokes.setup: Bart using childish euphemism for urination
jokes.punchline: Gina's nostalgic preference for harder time over Bart's company
jokes.explanation: Gina finding Bart so annoying she prefers actual prison to juvenile detention with him

jokes.text: If they're smart, kent, they'll stay off the main roads. It's all here in my book-- 'ten habits of highly successful criminals.'
jokes.setup: Snake providing expert criminal advice on TV
jokes.punchline: Shamelessly plugging his self-help book for criminals
jokes.explanation: Snake's criminal expertise commodified into self-help book parody

jokes.text: Tell them I'll be on conan thursday, with heather locklear and third-eye blind.
jokes.setup: Snake promoting his book appearance
jokes.punchline: Criminal getting mainstream talk show booking with 90s celebrities
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity booking for career criminal promoting crime advice

jokes.text: Why would bart escape if he's going to be released in two weeks? You just don't understand boys. He's stupid.
jokes.setup: News anchor asking logical question about Bart's escape timing
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally simple explanation of his son's decision-making
jokes.explanation: Homer's matter-of-fact assessment of his son's intelligence on live TV

jokes.text: You shall marry my daughter, moonhilda. I can't wait to lay my eggs in his brain.
jokes.setup: TV show with alien marriage subplot
jokes.punchline: Alien reproduction involving brain egg-laying
jokes.explanation: Standard alien horror trope with reproductive body horror

jokes.text: So why'd they send you to juvie? Shoplift a scrunchie? Get bent. I pushed snow white over the parapet at disneyland.
jokes.setup: Bart assuming petty female crime
jokes.punchline: Gina's assault on Disney character
jokes.explanation: Escalation from petty theft assumption to violent assault on beloved character

jokes.text: Oh, man. Did she live? Yeah, but it's not a good life.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about victim's survival
jokes.punchline: Gina's implication of permanent disability
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about permanent consequences of Gina's violent act

jokes.text: I don't have such a good memory since I drank my thermometer... but I whittles what I sees.
jokes.setup: Cletus explaining his memory problems
jokes.punchline: His artistic documentation method despite brain damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus character work - his simple-minded solutions and mercury poisoning

jokes.text: Uh, hey, what are you making now? Uh, sometimes I whittles the future.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking about Cletus's current carving
jokes.punchline: Cletus claiming prophetic woodworking abilities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Cletus's whittling from documentation to prediction

jokes.text: but there's no such thing as cooties, cootie shots, cootie force-fields, or cootie insurance. But state farm took my money.
jokes.setup: Gina explaining cooties aren't real
jokes.punchline: Bart's claim about insurance fraud
jokes.explanation: Escalation from childhood misconceptions to corporate insurance scam

jokes.text: You ke your ink hole shut about this.
jokes.setup: Family member demanding secrecy about their criminal lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Creative mispronunciation/euphemism for mouth
jokes.explanation: Crude attempt at creative profanity substitute

jokes.text: Take it from a blacksmith-- that girl's trouble. Oh, that girl's nuts. First she likes me then she hates me. I've already forgotten how ugly she is.
jokes.setup: Blacksmith warning Bart about Gina, Bart agreeing about her instability
jokes.punchline: Bart's shallow focus on her appearance
jokes.explanation: Bart's typical shallow male perspective reducing complex female behavior to looks

jokes.text: Wait... do you even have a family? No, I don't! They're imaginary, like your brain!
jokes.setup: Bart discovering Gina's family situation
jokes.punchline: Gina's defensive insult about imaginary concepts
jokes.explanation: Gina's emotional outburst with creative insult parallel

jokes.text: you're such a psycho! Mama's boy! future skank! Family guy!
jokes.setup: Bart and Gina trading insults
jokes.punchline: Family guy as ultimate insult from someone without family
jokes.explanation: Escalating insult fight with Gina's envy of family connections

jokes.text: Well, well, well. If it isn't punch n' juvie. Plant the evidence on them, boys. Uh, chief, we don't have to. These ones are actually guilty.
jokes.setup: Wiggum arriving to arrest the escapees
jokes.punchline: His routine corruption being unnecessary for once
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum corruption reveal - his default assumption of evidence planting

jokes.text: Super. Makes our job that much easier.
jokes.setup: Learning they don't need to plant evidence
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's casual satisfaction with legitimate police work
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's relief at not having to do illegal police work for once

jokes.text: Son, I want you to know, no matter what anyone says, there's no shame in being caught alive.
jokes.setup: Homer offering paternal comfort
jokes.punchline: Implying death would have been more honorable than capture
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer parenting - accidentally suggesting death would be more honorable

jokes.text: He will have a great bod, though. And a couple of those teardrop tattoos. Those are cool.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing Bart's future after prison
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm for prison body modifications
jokes.explanation: Homer's misguided appreciation for prison culture aesthetics

jokes.text: I wouldn't feel right going to the bathroom with no one watching.
jokes.setup: Gina commenting on new cellmates
jokes.punchline: Her comfort with complete lack of privacy
jokes.explanation: Gina's institutionalization making privacy feel abnormal

jokes.text: Gina, thanks for showing us the meaning of christmas. And thanks for showing my brother that girls can be cool. Who is this nerd?
jokes.setup: Lisa giving heartfelt thanks to Gina
jokes.punchline: Gina's immediate dismissal of Lisa
jokes.explanation: Gina's lack of sentimentality contrasting with Lisa's earnestness

jokes.text: Well, my shift's over. I guess it's back to my bachelor apartment. Make a tuna sandwich, turn on will and grace and cry myself to sleep.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his evening plans
jokes.punchline: His pathetic routine of loneliness and emotional TV
jokes.explanation: Homer's surprisingly vulnerable admission of his lonely evening routine

tmdb_overview: Bart gets sent to juvenile hall after fooling everyone that there was a marriage concerning him. He encounters a dangerous girl there... He flees with Gina, but then the two are caught by Wiggum.
